pattern: The OR of a range can only equal one of its own members when that member's bits already cover every bit anyone else in the range contributes, and as a range's left edge slides, its OR only ever gains bits -- a fact that keeps the number of distinct OR values, and therefore the work, small.
hints: A subarray's OR equals one of its own elements exactly when some element in it already has every bit that the OR has -- so scan for, per right edge, which OR value each possible left edge produces.

As the left edge of a fixed-right-edge window moves further left, the OR value never decreases and only changes O(log(max value)) times, since each change must set a new bit -- group consecutive left edges that share an OR value together instead of testing each one.

For a group of left edges sharing OR value V, the subarray is good only if V itself occurs as an element within that window -- track the most recent position where each value last appeared and compare it against the group's boundaries.

edge_inputs.description: single element, always good on its own
all zeros, every subarray's OR is zero and zero is always present
identical repeated value, every subarray is good
strictly increasing powers of two, no subarray of length > 1 is good
a dominant value that already contains every other value's bits appears first, then smaller values
a dominant value appears last instead of first
value at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random small-range integers, likely to create many OR collisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 63) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: random wide-range integers exercising the full bit width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
